projects.name: OmniParse
projects.description: OmniParse is a platform that ingests and parses any unstructured data into structured, actionable data optimized for GenAI (LLM) applications. Whether you are working with documents, tables, images, videos, audio files, or web pages, OmniParse prepares your data to be clean, structured, and ready for AI applications such as RAG, fine-tuning, and more
projects.short_description: Ingest, parse, and optimize any data format тЮбя╕П from documents to multimedia тЮбя╕П for enhanced compatibility with GenAI frameworks

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Omniparse - An open-source framework that scales the conversion of unstructured data into structured data. It is designed to handle a wide variety of data formats, offering unparalleled flexibility and scalability.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: One lesson IтАЩd tell my younger self: You canтАЩt always build something big in a weekend, but you can build something big by committing every weekend. Be patient, stay consistent, and trust the process.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: IтАЩve learned so much from reading and understanding open-source codeтАФit's where I picked up best practices, libraries, and real-world problem solving. Maintaining FOSS projects is my way of giving back to that community. It also lets me build high-impact tools that people actually use, and it's one of the fastest ways to validate and iterate on startup ideas in the real world.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response: Never Gonna Give You Up by Rick Astley тАз 1987

form.question: Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: currently the entire v1 of omniparse , v2 coming out soon

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: I built tools to solve my own problems, shared them online, and realized others found them useful too. ThatтАЩs how my open-source journey and Cognitivelab began.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
